temperature.note: Stable, within target range. 24hr mean 76.97°F, minimal drift.

ph.note: Acceptable. Slight upward trend (+0.18 in 24h) likely ammonia buffering—normal for active cycle.

tds.note: Healthy. Mid-range, stable. Declining 1ppm/24h indicates bioload balance.

reasoning: Cycle is progressing aggressively: ammonia 1.0ppm (spike phase), nitrite 2.0ppm (concerning but expected mid-cycle), nitrate 80ppm (good colonization). Chemistry is stable and within spec; concern is cycle intensity and whether it peaks safely before shrimp arrival.
recommended_actions: Continue daily observation of ammonia/nitrite. Cycle should shift in 3-5 days.
If ammonia exceeds 2ppm or nitrite >5ppm at next test, do 25% water change.
Monitor pH—acidification risk if cycle accelerates. Current uptrend is reassuring.
Hold water changes unless spike detected; bioload drives cycle maturation.
suggested_actuator_actions: None. Manual observation adequate. Await next test event.
